{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy/issues/7820","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy/issues/7820/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy/issues/7820/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy/issues/7820/events","html_url":"https://github.com/sunpy/sunpy/issues/7820","id":2560941819,"node_id":"I_kwDOACEKh86YpN77","number":7820,"title":"SOAR Solar Distance Query: Fido Error ","user":{"login":"esdcheliodevops","id":113588315,"node_id":"U_kgDOBsU4Ww","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/113588315?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/esdcheliodevops","html_url":"https://github.com/esdcheliodevops","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/esdcheliodevops/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/esdcheliodevops/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/esdcheliodevops/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/esdcheliodevops/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/esdcheliodevops/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/esdcheliodevops/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/esdcheliodevops/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/esdcheliodevops/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/esdcheliodevops/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-10-02T08:20:37Z","updated_at":"2024-10-02T08:20:37Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"### Describe the bug

I am encountering an issue with the SOAR solar distance functionality. Here's the relevant documentation link: 

[https://docs.sunpy.org/projects/soar/generated/gallery/solar_distance_query.html](url)

Before proceeding, I needed to download a specific version because the distance class is not present in sunpy.net.attributes. To resolve this, I checked out this specific commit: https://github.com/sunpy/sunpy-soar/commit/37d1227f0d3341de42413e4475124e6fba36f40d and built it from there.

However, after building, I encountered an internal error related to Fido.

**Steps to reproduce:**

-  Download and build from commit 37d1227f0d3341de42413e4475124e6fba36f40d.
-  Attempt to use the solar distance functionality as documented.

**Expected behavior:** The functionality should work as described in the documentation.

**Actual behavior:** An internal error occurs related to Fido.

### To Reproduce


`
import astropy.units as u
import sunpy.net.attrs as a
from sunpy.net import Fido

import sunpy_soar  # NOQA: F401

import sys
import os

# Add the path to the sunpy_soar directory
sunpy_soar_path = '/Users/....'
sys.path.insert(0, sunpy_soar_path)

# Now try importing the Distance class
from attrs import Distance  # Adjust based on where Distance is defined

instrument = a.Instrument("EUI")
time = a.Time("2020-10-29 05:00:00", "2022-10-29 06:00:00")
level = a.Level(2)
detector = a.Detector("HRI_EUV")
distance = Distance(0.45 * u.AU, 0.46 * u.AU)

query = instrument & time & level & detector & distance
result = Fido.search(instrument & level & detector & distance)
`

### Screenshots

![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/4fec1fb6-d202-47b8-9d6c-08fb86e919df)


### System Details

Astropy version: 6.1.4
SunPy version: 6.0.2
Python version: 3.12.3
sunpy_soar version: 1.11.dev20+g37d1227
macOS 14.6.0


### Installation method

[https://docs.sunpy.org/projects/soar/generated/gallery/solar_distance_query.html](url)

Before proceeding, I needed to download a specific version because the distance class is not present in sunpy.net.attributes. To resolve this, I checked out this specific commit: https://github.com/sunpy/sunpy-soar/commit/37d1227f0d3341de42413e4475124e6fba36f40d and built it from there.

However, after building, I encountered an internal error related to Fido.

**Steps to reproduce:**

-  Download and build from commit 37d1227f0d3341de42413e4475124e6fba36f40d.
-  Attempt to use the solar distance functionality as documented.

**Expected behavior:** The functionality should work as described in the documentation.

**Actual behavior:** An internal error occurs related to Fido.

### To Reproduce


```python
import astropy.units as u
import sunpy.net.attrs as a
from sunpy.net import Fido

import sunpy_soar  # NOQA: F401

import sys
import os

sunpy_soar_path = '/Users/....'

from attrs import Distance  # Adjust based on where Distance is defined

level = a.Level(2)
detector = a.Detector("HRI_EUV")
distance = Distance(0.45 * u.AU, 0.46 * u.AU)

query = instrument & time & level & detector & distance
```

{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy-soar/issues/46","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy-soar","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy-soar/issues/46/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy-soar/issues/46/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy-soar/issues/46/events","html_url":"https://github.com/sunpy/sunpy-soar/issues/46","id":1349654925,"node_id":"I_kwDOFPQQCs5QchmN","number":46,"title":"Query SOAR metadata","user":{"login":"ebuchlin","id":3624676,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjM2MjQ2NzY=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/3624676?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/ebuchlin","html_url":"https://github.com/ebuchlin","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ebuchlin/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ebuchlin/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ebuchlin/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ebuchlin/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ebuchlin/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ebuchlin/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ebuchlin/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ebuchlin/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ebuchlin/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[{"id":2856162101,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwyODU2MTYyMTAx","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy-soar/labels/enhancement","name":"enhancement","color":"a2eeef","default":true,"description":"New feature or request"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":18,"created_at":"2022-08-24T15:42:26Z","updated_at":"2024-06-20T07:19:35Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"### Describe the feature

My understanding is that `sunpy-soar` currently only supports queries by instrument / time / level / product, as this is basically what is available in the [SOAR web query form](http://soar.esac.esa.int/soar/#search) and in the `v_sc_data_item` and `v_ll_data_item` tables. However, the user should also be able to do queries with different metadata (other Fido attributes).


### Proposed solution

The [list of all tables and their columns](http://soar.esac.esa.int/soar-sl-tap/tap/tables) is available from SOAR with TAP. I attach a  [human-readable version](https://github.com/sunpy/sunpy-soar/files/9417491/tables.md) (tree by schema / table / column), generated by XSLT with [this XSL stylesheet](https://github.com/sunpy/sunpy-soar/files/9417512/tables-md.txt).

This shows that more complete metadata are available in SOAR, in instrument-specific tables, e.g. `v_spi_sc_fits`. Example query: http://soar.esac.esa.int/soar-sl-tap/tap//sync?REQUEST=doQuery&LANG=ADQL&FORMAT=json&QUERY=SELECT+TOP+10+%2A+FROM+v_spi_sc_fits

Fido attributes should be linked to columns in these different instrument-specific tables. For a query with multiple instruments, multiple tables should be queried... (or should this not be supported?). LL files metadata can also be queried, from still different tables.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy-soar/issues/46/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy-soar/issues/46/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy-soar/issues/comments/2179987714","html_url":"https://github.com/sunpy/sunpy-soar/issues/46#issuecomment-2179987714","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy-soar/issues/46","id":2179987714,"node_id":"IC_kwDOFPQQCs6B7_kC","user":{"login":"esdcheliodevops","id":113588315,"node_id":"U_kgDOBsU4Ww","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/113588315?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/esdcheliodevops","html_url":"https://github.com/esdcheliodevops","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/esdcheliodevops/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/esdcheliodevops/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/esdcheliodevops/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/esdcheliodevops/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/esdcheliodevops/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/esdcheliodevops/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/esdcheliodevops/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/esdcheliodevops/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/esdcheliodevops/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-06-20T07:19:34Z","updated_at":"2024-06-20T07:19:34Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"Hello @ebuchlin and @hayesla - I would just like to comment that if you find the current structure of Tables in SOAR TAP difficult to work with, then please do make suggestions about how we can improve that. :-)

They are currently structured with the internal relational database in mind. But of course, we are always open to the possibility of making more user friendly views to combine data etc. This would help avoid making complex queries with joins which are often slow due to lack of indexes etc on certain columns.

It would be great to capture this kind of feedback which would surely benefit the whole community of SOAR TAP users.

Many thanks,
